summary: SEPA Payout System - All 8 features tested successfully. Backend APIs (pending, create, process, history, batch-process, my-pending) working correctly. Admin Panel SEPA-Auszahlungen tab visible and functional with statistics display. Fixed bug in enterprise payout endpoints (my-pending, my-history) that used incorrect token lookup.

3_api_process_payout.note: SEPA transfer is SIMULATED - generates reference but does not connect to real bank

6_api_enterprise_my_pending.bug_fixed: Token lookup was using enterprise_accounts.token instead of enterprise_sessions

bug_fixed.description: Enterprise payout endpoints (my-pending, my-history) were using incorrect token lookup
bug_fixed.root_cause: Code was looking for token in enterprise_accounts.token field, but login creates sessions in enterprise_sessions collection
bug_fixed.fix_applied: Changed both endpoints to use get_enterprise_from_token() helper function which correctly looks up sessions

seed_data_creation: Test payouts created during testing (TEST_payout_creation, TEST_process_payout)

mocked_apis_list: SEPA bank transfer - simulated with reference number (SEPA-XXXXXXXX format)
mocked_apis.note: In production, this would integrate with a bank API like Wise, Deutsche Bank, or similar SEPA provider

context_for_next_testing_agent: SEPA payout system complete. Admin endpoints: GET /api/enterprise/admin/payouts/pending (list pending), POST /create (create payout), POST /{id}/process (process with SEPA ref), GET /history (with filters), POST /batch-process (auto-process due). Enterprise endpoints: GET /payouts/my-pending (own pending amount), GET /payouts/my-history (own history). AdminPayouts.js component shows statistics and allows manual/batch processing. SEPA transfer is SIMULATED - generates reference number but does not connect to real bank API.
